title: Canada Study Permit Journey
description: Step-by-step roadmap for Pakistani students applying for a Canadian Study Permit — from DLI acceptance through arriving in Canada and maintaining your study permit conditions.
disclaimer: Informational guidance only. Canadian immigration rules change frequently. Always confirm current requirements at IRCC – Study Permit before taking any action.
info: Canada issues a Study Permit rather than a traditional student visa. Pakistani nationals are not visa-exempt and also require a Temporary Resident Visa (TRV), which IRCC typically processes together with the study permit application. You must be accepted by a Designated Learning Institution (DLI). Review current requirements at canada.ca.

steps.name: Choose a Designated Learning Institution (DLI)
steps.description: You must be accepted by a school on the official IRCC list of Designated Learning Institutions. Not every Canadian school qualifies — verify your target institution before applying.
actions: Search the official DLI list at canada.ca and confirm your institution is listed
Note the DLI number associated with your institution — required for your study permit application
Research programs matching your academic background and career goals
Confirm whether a study permit is needed: programs of six months or less may not require one — check current rules at canada.ca
Review the institution's language requirements and application deadlines
steps.output: Target DLI confirmed on the official IRCC DLI list; DLI number recorded

steps.name: Apply and Receive Your DLI Acceptance Letter
steps.description: Submit your application to the DLI and obtain an official letter of acceptance. This document is a mandatory requirement for the study permit application.
actions: Submit a complete application to your chosen DLI well before their stated deadline
Provide all required academic transcripts, language test results, and supporting documents
Upon admission: receive your official letter of acceptance (offer of admission)
Verify the letter contains your name, program name, program start date, and DLI name and number
Keep the original — it will be uploaded to your IRCC online application
steps.output: Official DLI acceptance letter received with correct student and program details

steps.name: Confirm Your Passport is Valid
steps.description: You must have a valid passport to apply. Pakistani nationals also require a Temporary Resident Visa (TRV), which IRCC typically processes alongside the study permit application.
actions: Confirm your Pakistani passport is valid and will remain valid for the duration of your studies in Canada
Ensure sufficient blank pages remain
If renewal is needed: renew well in advance before starting the study permit application
Prepare a clear scan of your passport biographical page for upload
steps.output: Valid passport confirmed; clear scan prepared for upload

steps.name: Gather Proof of Financial Support
steps.description: You must demonstrate to IRCC that you have sufficient funds to cover tuition, living expenses, and your return journey. Required amounts are set by IRCC and updated periodically.
steps.canadaNote: Do not rely on unofficial sources for financial amounts — they can change. Check the current financial proof requirements at canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/study-permit/get-documents.html#financial before gathering documents.
actions: Check the current required funds at the IRCC financial requirements page
Gather recent official bank statements covering a period confirmed on the IRCC website
If a parent or sponsor is supporting you: include their bank statements and a signed support letter
If you hold a scholarship: include the award letter as financial evidence
Ensure all financial documents are in English or French, or include a certified translation
steps.output: Financial evidence assembled meeting current IRCC requirements

steps.name: Gather Supporting Documents
steps.description: IRCC generates a personalised document checklist based on your application profile. Review the full list on the IRCC website for your specific situation before gathering documents.
actions: Review the full IRCC document checklist at canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/study-permit/get-documents.html
Prepare two recent passport-size photographs meeting IRCC photo specifications
Gather academic transcripts and diplomas demonstrating your educational background
Prepare a study plan or statement of purpose if recommended by IRCC guidance
Check whether a police certificate is currently required for Pakistani applicants on the IRCC website
steps.output: All required supporting documents gathered and ready for upload

steps.name: Create a GCKey Account and Start the Application
steps.description: Canadian study permit applications are submitted online through the IRCC secure portal using a GCKey or Sign-In Partner government account.
actions: Go to canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/study-permit/apply.html and follow the online application link
Create a GCKey account at canada.ca/en/government/sign-in-online-account/gckey.html if you do not have one
Complete the IRCC eligibility questionnaire to confirm the correct permit type
Begin a new study permit application — Pakistani nationals will also be prompted for a Temporary Resident Visa (TRV)
steps.output: IRCC online account created; study permit and TRV application started

steps.name: Complete the Online Application Form
steps.description: Fill in the IRCC form accurately using your DLI acceptance details, personal information, and travel history. Every field must match your supporting documents exactly.
actions: Enter your DLI name, DLI number, and program details as on your acceptance letter
Provide complete personal information matching your passport exactly
Answer all background and eligibility questions honestly and completely
Upload all documents as specified in your personalised IRCC document checklist
Review every section for errors before proceeding to payment
steps.output: Online application form completed; all documents uploaded

steps.name: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the study permit application fee (and TRV fee if applicable) as part of the online application. Fees are non-refundable. Verify current amounts on the official IRCC fee page.
steps.warn: Do not rely on unofficial sources for current fee amounts. Check current fees at ircc.canada.ca before paying. Fees are generally non-refundable even if the application is refused.
actions: Check current study permit and TRV fees at ircc.canada.ca/english/information/fees/index.asp
Pay all applicable fees online using a valid credit or debit card
Save your payment confirmation receipt before submitting
steps.output: Application fee and TRV fee paid; payment confirmation saved

steps.name: Receive Biometric Instruction Letter (BIL)
steps.description: After submitting your application, IRCC will issue a Biometric Instruction Letter if biometrics are required. Pakistani nationals are typically required to provide biometrics.
actions: Monitor your IRCC online account and registered email for the Biometric Instruction Letter (BIL)
Note the biometrics deadline stated in the BIL — you must enrol within this timeframe
Confirm whether existing biometrics on file with IRCC are still valid (biometrics have a validity period — check with IRCC)
If biometrics are required: proceed to book at a VFS Global VAC in Pakistan

steps.name: Book and Attend VFS Global Biometrics Appointment
steps.description: IRCC directs Pakistani applicants to book biometrics at Visa Application Centres (VACs) operated by VFS Global in Pakistan. Book through the VFS Global Canada Pakistan portal.
steps.canadaNote: VFS Global is the official service provider for Canadian biometrics in Pakistan. Book your appointment at canada.vfsglobal.com/pak/en/. VACs are located in Islamabad, Karachi, and Lahore — confirm current locations and availability on the VFS website.
actions: Visit canada.vfsglobal.com/pak/en/ to book a biometrics appointment at the nearest VAC
Select the VAC location appropriate to your city of residence
Book an available appointment date and time
Bring your Biometric Instruction Letter (printed or on your phone), valid passport, and any additional documents specified by VFS
Attend the appointment and provide fingerprints and photograph as directed
Collect your receipt confirming biometrics enrollment
steps.output: Biometrics enrolled at VFS Global VAC in Pakistan within the BIL deadline

steps.name: Await and Receive the Decision
steps.description: IRCC will process your study permit and TRV and issue a decision. Processing times vary — check current estimates on the IRCC website. Avoid non-refundable travel commitments until you receive a positive outcome.
steps.warn: Do not book non-refundable flights until your study permit is approved. Check current processing times at canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/application/check-processing-times.html.
actions: Check current study permit processing times on the IRCC website
Monitor your IRCC online account and email for updates or requests for additional documents
Respond promptly to any IRCC request for further information
If approved: you will receive a Port of Entry (POE) Letter of Introduction; Pakistani nationals will also receive a TRV stamp in their passport
If refused: review the refusal reasons carefully and assess your options
steps.output: Study permit approval and POE Letter of Introduction received

steps.name: Prepare to Travel to Canada
steps.description: Your study permit is not affixed to your passport — it is issued as a separate document by a CBSA officer at the Canadian port of entry. Carry your POE Letter of Introduction when you travel.
actions: Confirm you have your Port of Entry (POE) Letter of Introduction from IRCC
Confirm your TRV stamp is in your valid passport
Book flights arriving before your program start date and within your TRV validity
Carry your DLI acceptance letter, financial evidence, and POE Letter in your hand luggage
Review entry expectations at canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/study-permit/prepare-arrival.html
steps.output: Travel booked; all required documents prepared for the Canadian port of entry

steps.name: Clear Canadian Border and Receive Your Study Permit
steps.description: Present your POE Letter of Introduction to the CBSA officer at the Canadian port of entry. The officer will issue your physical study permit document on arrival.
actions: Present your valid passport, valid TRV, and POE Letter of Introduction to the CBSA officer
Carry your DLI acceptance letter and financial evidence in case they are requested
Answer all CBSA officer questions honestly
Receive your study permit document — review it immediately for accuracy (name, DLI, program, validity dates)
If any details are incorrect: raise this with the CBSA officer before leaving the port of entry
steps.output: Study permit issued at Canadian port of entry; all details verified as correct

steps.name: Enrol at Your DLI
steps.description: Complete enrolment and registration at your DLI as required. Your study permit conditions require you to actively study at the DLI named on your permit.
actions: Attend your DLI's orientation, enrolment, and registration sessions
Complete all registration requirements by the DLI's stated deadline
Confirm your program and enrolment details match what is on your study permit
If you need to change DLI or program: check IRCC requirements for updating your study permit before making any change
steps.output: Enrolled and registered at the DLI named on your study permit

steps.name: Understand and Comply with Study Permit Conditions
steps.description: Your study permit conditions are printed on the permit document and set by IRCC. Violating conditions can result in permit cancellation and future immigration consequences.
steps.canadaNote: Study permit conditions include a requirement to actively pursue studies and remain enrolled at a DLI. Work eligibility rules (on- and off-campus) depend on your specific permit conditions — check current work rules at canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/work.html. Do not work beyond permitted limits.
actions: Read all conditions printed on your study permit document
Understand the active study requirement: you must be genuinely pursuing studies at your DLI
Check current work eligibility rules for study permit holders at canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/study-canada/work.html
Do not work without authorisation or beyond permitted limits
If your circumstances change (change of school, program, or address): check IRCC requirements for permit updates
Track your study permit expiry date and apply to extend before it expires
steps.output: Study permit conditions understood; studying and working within all permitted limits

documents: Proof of Financial Support (Bank Statements, Scholarship Letters, etc.)
Study Plan or Statement of Purpose (if required)
